meta.subject: Programming Challenges
meta.description: Coding problems with test cases - Easy level

questions.content: Write a function to check if a string is a palindrome. A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards, ignoring case and spaces.
questions.subject: Programming Challenges
questions.code_template:
def is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool:
    """
    Check if a string is a palindrome.
    Ignore case and spaces.
    
    Examples:
        is_palindrome("racecar") -> True
        is_palindrome("hello") -> False
        is_palindrome("A man a plan a canal Panama") -> True
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

test_cases.input: A man a plan a canal Panama
Was it a car or a cat I saw

hints: Remove spaces and convert to lowercase first
Compare the string with its reverse
You can use slicing s[::-1] to reverse a string

questions.content: Implement a function that returns the factorial of a non-negative integer n.
questions.subject: Programming Challenges
questions.code_template:
def factorial(n: int) -> int:
    """
    Calculate factorial of n.
    
    Examples:
        factorial(5) -> 120
        factorial(0) -> 1
        factorial(1) -> 1
    """

    pass

questions.content: Write a function to find the maximum element in a list of integers.
questions.subject: Programming Challenges
questions.code_template:
def find_max(nums: list) -> int:
    """
    Find the maximum element in a list.
    Assume the list is not empty.
    
    Examples:
        find_max([1, 5, 3, 9, 2]) -> 9
        find_max([10]) -> 10
    """

    pass
hints: Track the maximum value as you iterate
Initialize max with the first element
Python has a built-in max() function, but try implementing it yourself
